input.prompt:
I have two ASP.NET Core 8 services communicating via RabbitMQ. Service A publishes
an order event and Service B consumes it. Traces show up fine within each service
but I can't see the end-to-end distributed trace across the queue boundary.
How do I propagate the trace context through message headers? Don't create files.

input.initialPrompt:
I have two ASP.NET Core 8 services communicating via RabbitMQ. Service A publishes
an order event and Service B consumes it. Traces show up fine within each service
but I can't see the end-to-end distributed trace across the queue boundary.

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about distributed tracing context propagation across RabbitMQ message boundaries in ASP.NET Core 8. They want to see end-to-end traces across Service A (publisher) and Service B (consumer). They don't want me to create files — just explain the approach.

This is a knowledge question, not a code task. I'll give a concise but complete answer.

The key concept: RabbitMQ doesn't automatically propagate W3C trace context. You need to manually inject the trace context into message headers on the publisher side and extract it on the consumer side, then start a new activity linked to the extracted context.
 
For .NET 8, I'll focus on using `Activity` and `ActivitySource` from `System.Diagnostics` along with `DistributedContextPropagator` to serialize the trace context into RabbitMQ message headers and deserialize it back out on the consumer end.
